mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows or positive returns occur in less than 50% of low-rate observations.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 6.5%, consumer spending growth stagnates and corporate earnings forecasts are downgraded, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 6.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts signal broad-based consumer demand expansion, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Joint positive changes in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive returns with a statistical significance level of p less than 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates strengthening manufacturing output and supply chain utilization, which translates to positive short-term S&P 500 returns during economic expansion phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to generate positive 5-day returns during late-cycle expansions, with a hit rate below 45%.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding labor demand and rising household income, which consistently supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Payroll growth shows no statistically significant correlation with 20-day forward returns across multiple market cycles.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index crosses above 235.0, anticipated monetary tightening and margin compression trigger negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in more than 55% of periods where CPIAUCSL exceeds 235.0.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent quarterly declines in real GDP and monthly increases in the unemployment rate signal an economic contraction that reliably produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined signal of falling GDPC1 and rising UNRATE fails to predict negative returns with a directional accuracy above 50%.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in industrial production combined with falling retail sales indicates weakening aggregate demand and factory utilization, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Negative returns do not materialize in more than 60% of triggered instances where both series decline simultaneously.
